texto: They do say money is the root of all evil."I thought that was supposed to be the love of money."There's neat for you. 'Tis them without it that loves it best.

texto: —Help these boys build a nation their own. Ransack the histories for clues to their past. Plunder the literatures for words they can speak. And should you encounter an ancient tribe whose customs, however dimly, cast light on their hearts, tell them that tale; and you shall name the unspeakable names of your kind, and in that naming, in each such telling, they will falter a step to the light."—For only with pride may a man prosper. With pride, all things follow. Without he have pride he is a shadowy skulk whose season is night.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Jamie O'Neill sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “—Help these boys build a nation their own. Ransack the histories for clues to their past. Plunder…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, mentoring, pride, útil em momentos de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

texto: My black-headed black-eyed boy. I remember every day of you. How would I forget?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jamie O'Neill que provoca temas ligados a familia. Ao questionar “My black-headed black-eyed boy. I remember every day of you. How would I forget?”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, family, memory, com foco em memory.

texto: Grey morning dulled the bay. Banks of clouds, Howth just one more bank, rolled to sea, where other Howths grumbled to greet them. Swollen spumeless tide. Heads that bobbed like floating gulls and gulls that floating bobbed like heads. Two heads. At swim, two boys.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jamie O'Neill: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Grey morning dulled the bay. Banks of clouds, Howth just one more bank, rolled to sea, where othe…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, ireland, sea pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e sea.
